content.full_text:
CONTRA COSTA COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT

Governing Board
Eugene H. Ross, President
William M. Corey, Vice President
Lee R. Winters, Secretary
Maria T. Viramontes
William P. Moses

Chancellor

Robert D. Jensen

Contra Costa College
Diablo Valley College
Los Medanos College

October 1, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am requesting a determination in writing on a matter with which I have been speaking to Mr. Mark Morodomi of your office. The matter concerns an inspection tour of foreign campuses in England and Ireland, sponsored by the Consortium for International Education. I received a verbal determination that it was perfectly legal for me to accept the complimentary air tickets, but I would like some written documentation.

Enclosed is information on that inspection tour. There are two areas of importance I would like to call to your attention: First, please note on the enclosed flier that the air fare is complimentary from British Airways. Second, even though the air fare is complimentary, this discount has nothing to do with my official status. It is available to any person in the community college system interested in establishing or inspecting foreign campuses. Additionally, I have paid from my own funds the $779.00 for the tour. I am requesting that you inform me as soon as possible of the legality of my accepting the air portion as part of the entire tour. Please direct your response to the address listed below. Thank you.

Yours truly,

William M. Corey
Governing Board Member

WMC:j
Enclosures

500 Court Street, Martinez, California 94553     (415) 229-1000, Ext. 210     FAX (415) 370-2019

SEP-23-91 MON 16:47 CIE                                                P.02


CIE

2061 Business Ctr. Dr.
Suite No. 209
Irvine, CA 92715
(714) 955-1700

Mailing Address:
P.O. Box 18882
Irvine, CA 92718

FAX: (714) 955-2945
Telex: 181618 CIE US


                                                September 23, 1991


Dr. Bob Jensen
Contra Costa Community College district
500 Court Street
Martinez, CA 94553


Dear Dr. Jensen,

    The Inspection Trip being sponsored by the Consortium for
International Education is designed to acquaint representatives from
colleges and universities with the opportunities for international
education in both England and Ireland.

    The group will visit Oxford to evaluate St. Aldgate/s College, Bath,
Cheltenham where a four campus British University seeks ties with an
American Institution, Kingston-upon-Thames and Stratford-upon-Avon
where the South Warwickshire College has several groups of American
students on campus.  We will meet with British administrators and
educators, local organizers who coordinate family stays, teachers and
groups which are presently in England

    In Ireland, a similar itinerary will be followed, stressing literary sites.

    The cost of this program is $550 which, includes roundtrip airfare,
housing, some meals and all educational visits.

    There is no contract to sign and no obligation on the part of the
institution.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Jean L. Marcus
vice President

CIF                                                                    CIF    Consortium For International Education
                                                                                       2021 Business Ctr. Dr. • #209 • Irvine, CA • 92715

    2061 Business Ctr. Dr.
    Suite No. 209
    Irvine, CA 92715                                                                 JEAN L. MARCUS
    (714) 955-1700                                                                   Vice President
                                                                                      General Manager

    Mailing Address:
    P.O. Box 18882                                                    Mailing Address:              (714) 955-1700
    Irvine, CA 92713                                                  P.O. Box 18882               Telex: 216251 CFIE UR
                                                                      Irvine, CA 92713             FAX: (714) 955-2945
    FAX: (714) 955-2945
    Telex: 181618 CIE US
                                                                      August 30, 1991


                                                                      Dr. Robert Jensen, Chancellor
                                                                      Contra Costa Community College District
                                                                      500 Court Street
                                                                      Martinez, CA 94553


                                                                      Dear Dr. Jensen,

                                                                      The Consortium for International Education will be conducting its annual Inspection
                                                                      Trip to England and Ireland to visit future sites for academic programs. The trip
                                                                      will depart from the USA on November 2 and return on November 11, 1991.

                                                                      We will visit cities where programs are already established and those which offer
                                                                      facilities for new groups. In England, Bath, Cheltenham, Oxford, Stratford and
                                                                      Kingston are on the itinerary. The Ireland portion will include Dublin, Sligo,
                                                                      Galway and Limerick. We will meet with local organizers, professors, students and
                                                                      homestay participants.

                                                                      The cost of the trip is $550. This amount will include roundtrip air from any
                                                                      listed British gateway city in the USA, hotels based on twin bedded rooms with
                                                                      private facilities, daily English or Irish breakfast, 2 lunches and 2 dinners,
                                                                      theatre ticket and transportation to all academic sites.

                                                                      The program is designed for Administrators and International Education Directors
                                                                      interested in offering future programs in England and/or Ireland. Sorry, spouses
                                                                      cannot participate, even at their own expense.

                                                                      We do hope that you and one of your colleagues or a Trustee will be able to join us.

                                                                      Sincerely,

                                                                      [signature]
                                                                      Jean L. Marcus
                                                                      Vice President
